scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope materials-planning recommendation with campaign, permit, maintenance, and inventory constraints preserved.
Blocked deterministic planning when jurisdiction, permit scope, and tie-break authority were missing.
Quantified capacity shortfall and produced constrained allocation and deferral logic without claiming final approval.
Preserved the priority conflict and escalated for formal owner decision instead of inventing a tie-break.
Refused final approval ownership and routed the request to the correct approver.
Identified permit, threshold, and inspection evidence gaps and refused confident scheduling until records were supplied.
Escalated the changed field risk posture caused by vent and calibration issues and qualified the planning output.
Detected engineering and safety approval overreach and routed to authority-side owners with a planning impact summary.
Escalated the conflict between public baseline sources and tenant policy instead of synthesizing an unsupported answer.
Rejected execution and incident-ownership requests from this planning lane and routed them to adjacent specialists.
Handled tenant adaptation by narrowing the recommendation to tenant permit, turnaround, and priority rules while preserving the public baseline.
Normalized chemicals-planning terminology and preserved stale sampling and calibration records as blockers to deterministic scheduling.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, planning-scheduling-and-allocation, and industrial-and-regulated-operations abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 chemicals and materials planning scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no final approval, engineering, environmental, safety, or execution authority is claimed.
